data.name: AI 甜味蛋白研发 · sweet protein workflow

route_options_json.name: Single-chain monellin 定向改造
route_options_json.rationale: Single-chain monellin is the most documented sweet-protein scaffold with existing stability mutants (S76Y, W3C+R39G) and a reported GRAS notice (GRN 1269). It offers a clear path for partial diffusion redesign to reduce length below 100 aa while preserving the validated fold and known functional residues. Parallel expression hosts (yeast, T. reesei) and functional assays (human TAS1R2/TAS1R3) have precedent.
key_risks: Heat/processing stability may be insufficient for beverage pasteurization
Lingering sweetness or off-tastes are possible
Freedom-to-operate (FTO) requires review of GRN 1269 scope
required_evidence: Verify GRAS notice GRN 1269 status (FDA no-questions letter or pending)
Obtain crystal structure of single-chain monellin (PDB) for redesign
expected_benefits: High chance of retaining sweetness through core-stabilizing mutations
Cost-effective microbial production (P. pastoris, S. cerevisiae, T. reesei)
Shortest path to GRAS extension if notice status is favorable

route_options_json.name: Brazzein-53/54 定向改造
route_options_json.rationale: Brazzein is only 53-54 aa, inherently heat/acid stable, and has multiple GRAS notices (GRN 1207, GRN 1142). It is an ideal parallel scaffold for beverage applications. AI-directed redesign can target surface epitopes to reduce allergenicity risk while maintaining the fold and interactions with human TAS1R2/TAS1R3.
key_risks: Correct formation of four disulfide bonds in heterologous hosts
Crowded patent landscape may limit FTO
Expression host must be optimized for soluble, folded yield
required_evidence: Confirm GRAS notice status for GRN 1207 and GRN 1142
Identity of key receptor-binding residues on brazzein (literature)
High-resolution structure (NMR or X-ray) for design input
expected_benefits: Smallest size minimizes length constraint risk
Excellent thermal and pH stability for beverage processing
Existing GRAS notices provide regulatory precedent

route_options_json.name: pH-responsive 味觉修饰蛋白
route_options_json.rationale: pH-responsive modification (e.g., histidine-switch) could differentiate products for acidic beverages but introduces glycosylation, multi-subunit complexity, and regulatory uncertainty. Suitable as a second-generation track only if acid-dependent activation is a product differentiator.
key_risks: Glycosylation or multi-subunit requirements complicate production
No GRAS precedent for pH-responsive sweet proteins
required_evidence: Proprietary or published data on pH-responsive sweet protein variants
Proof of concept for acid beverage stability
expected_benefits: Unique market position for acidic drink applications
Potential for Intellectual Property (IP) if mechanism is novel

route_options_json.name: De novo 受体 binder
route_options_json.rationale: De novo design of a protein binder to TAS1R2/TAS1R3 is a high-risk strategy because receptor activation cannot be predicted from binding alone. No positive control for sweet-taste activation by a de novo protein exists, and no functional assay is defined. This route should only be executed after the receptor region, positive control, and functional assay are established.
key_risks: Predicted binding does not equate to receptor activation or sweetness
No expression or sensory validation data for de novo sweet protein binders
High screening cost with low probability of success
required_evidence: Defined receptor-binding site on TAS1R2/TAS1R3 (from structure or mutagenesis)
A positive control sweet protein that activates the receptor in assay
A validated functional assay (e.g., calcium flux) for receptor activation

seed.title: AI甜味蛋白_天然骨架_受体机制_计算设计与实验验证_2026-06-20
seed.verification_policy: Treat as a research seed. Verify recent papers, preprints, company claims, and regulatory status against primary or official sources.

receptor.name: human TAS1R2/TAS1R3
receptor.warning: Predicted binding does not establish activation or sweetness.
supported_regions: TAS1R3-TMD (allosteric modulation site for cyclamate, IMP)
uncertainties: Exact contact interface for sweet proteins (wedge-model) not yet structurally resolved
Role of TAS1R3-VFT in protein recognition is unclear
Coupling of VFT closure to TMD activation is not fully elucidated

verification_queue: Retrieve and review latest structure of human TAS1R2/TAS1R3 (cryo-EM, 10.1038/s41586-025-09302-6)
Confirm GRAS notice status for GRN 1269 (monellin), GRN 1207, GRN 1142 (brazzein) from FDA website
Obtain high-resolution structure of single-chain monellin (PDB) and brazzein (PDB)
Identify functional residues (wedge-model) through literature review
Establish contact with CRO for human TAS1R2/TAS1R3 functional assay
Run FTO preliminary search on monellin stabilization mutations and single-chain format

assumptions.value: GRN 1269 (modified monellin) has FDA no-questions letter
GRN 1207 and GRN 1142 have FDA no-questions letters
wedge-model residues from literature are correct
Pichia pastoris or S. cerevisiae suitable for initial screening

risks.risk: Truncation to <100 aa disrupts single-chain monellin fold or sweetness
risks.mitigation: Start with stability-enhanced mutants (S76Y, W3C+R39G) and trim loops based on B-factor and sequence conservation; test folding by SDS-PAGE and CD; test function by cell-based assay.

risks.risk: Disulfide bonding pattern disrupted by ProteinMPNN sequence design
risks.mitigation: Set 'omit_AAs': 'CX' and manually preserve cysteine residues; check disulfide connectivity with mass spectrometry after expression.

risks.risk: FTO conflict with existing monellin/brazzein patents or GRAS notices
risks.mitigation: Commission FTO search before first expression run; focus mutations on regions outside known IP claims.

risks.risk: Allergenicity from surface epitopes on redesigned monellin
risks.mitigation: Run allergenicity prediction (AllergenOnline, AlgPred) on each design; avoid introducing IgE-binding motifs; include in vitro digestibility assay.

risks.risk: Low expression yield or insolubility in initial host
risks.mitigation: Include codon optimization, secretion signal, and small-scale expression screening; have brazzein as parallel host candidate.

risks.risk: Receptor activation assay not available in-house
risks.mitigation: Identify CRO or academic collaborator with human TAS1R2/TAS1R3 calcium-flux assay before design start.

success_criteria.criterion: At least 50 designs predicted to fold with pLDDT > 85 and no disulfide breakage
success_criteria.evidence_required: AlphaFold2 multimer predictions for single-chain; pLDDT scores; disulfide geometry

success_criteria.criterion: ≥50% of selected designs express as soluble protein in P. pastoris or E. coli with yield ≥2 mg/L
success_criteria.evidence_required: SDS-PAGE, Western blots, and BCA assay

success_criteria.criterion: EC50 < 10 µM in human TAS1R2/TAS1R3 calcium-flux assay and maximal response > 50% of monellin WT
success_criteria.evidence_required: Concentration-response curves from cell-based assay with positive controls

success_criteria.criterion: Sweetness potency ≥ 1000x sucrose (weight basis) in trained panel with no offensive off-taste
success_criteria.evidence_required: Descriptive sensory analysis (n ≥ 8) with sucrose references

success_criteria.criterion: Sweetness retained after HTST pasteurization (72°C, 15 s) and 4-week storage in model beverage (pH 3.5) at 25°C
success_criteria.evidence_required: HPLC or sensor data showing <20% degradation; sensory triangle test

dossier_json.planning_summary: The Copilot selects single-chain monellin as the primary route and brazzein as a parallel backup. The computational workflow will focus on partial diffusion of sc-monellin to reduce length under 100 aa while preserving cysteine residues and functional residues identified from literature. ProteinMPNN will design sequences that maintain disulfide bonds and core stability. Designs will be filtered by AlphaFold2 pLDDT and allergenicity screens. Experimentally, designs will be expressed in P. pastoris and screened for solubility and folding, then tested in a human TAS1R2/TAS1R3 activation assay. Success is gated by receptor activation EC50 < 10 µM and sensory sweetness >1000x sucrose. Before design begins, key dependencies are confirmations of monellin structure, GRAS status, receptor contact residues, and assay availability.
